channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Artemis II astronauts have splashed down on Earth
item.description: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.content:encoded: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.dc:creator: Amina Khan

item.title: After more than 9 days in flight, NASA's Artemis II is set to return to Earth
item.description: The four astronauts on NASA's Artemis II lunar mission are set to return to Earth Friday evening. The crew is wrapping up a journey around the moon with a planned splashdown off San Diego, Calif.
item.content:encoded: The four astronauts on NASA's Artemis II lunar mission are set to return to Earth Friday evening. The crew is wrapping up a journey around the moon with a planned splashdown off San Diego, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Epstein survivors have mixed feelings on Melania Trump's call for hearing in Congress
item.description: The first lady made a public statement on Thursday saying she was not friends with Epstein, and calling for further action in Congress. Survivors of the late sex offender's abuse differ on her proposal.
item.content:encoded:
The first lady made a public statement on Thursday saying she was not friends with Epstein, and calling for further action in Congress. Survivors of the late sex offender's abuse differ on her proposal.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Months after the ICE shootings in Minnesota, a federal probe remains elusive
item.description: Officials in Minnesota have sued the Trump administration, saying federal officials are withholding evidence in the killings of U.S. citizens Alex Pretti and Renee Macklin Good by immigration agents in Minneapolis, as well as the non-fatal shooting of a Venezuelan man.
item.content:encoded:
Officials in Minnesota have sued the Trump administration, saying federal officials are withholding evidence in the killings of U.S. citizens Alex Pretti and Renee Macklin Good by immigration agents in Minneapolis, as well as the non-fatal shooting of a Venezuelan man.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Shortlisted for an Oscar, 'Homebound' is a daring movie about two dear friends
item.description: The movie, now streaming on Netflix, defied current trends in Indian cinema to tell the true story of a friendship between a Muslim and a Hindu Dalit. Martin Scorsese was secretly involved.
item.content:encoded:
The movie, now streaming on Netflix, defied current trends in Indian cinema to tell the true story of a friendship between a Muslim and a Hindu Dalit. Martin Scorsese was secretly involved.

(Image credit: Diaa Hadid/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Five things to know about Hungary's election
item.description: Hungary votes Sunday in a pivotal test of Viktor Orbán's "illiberal democracy," as challenger Péter Magyar taps voter frustration, with stakes for Europe, NATO and the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
Hungary votes Sunday in a pivotal test of Viktor Orbán's "illiberal democracy," as challenger Péter Magyar taps voter frustration, with stakes for Europe, NATO and the U.S.

(Image credit: Pool/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: 'How are you using AI?' Your therapist should ask you that question, experts argue
item.description: A paper in JAMA Psychiatry says mental health providers should ask if patients are using artificial intelligence chatbots, just as they would ask patients about sleep habits and substance use.
item.content:encoded:
A paper in JAMA Psychiatry says mental health providers should ask if patients are using artificial intelligence chatbots, just as they would ask patients about sleep habits and substance use.

(Image credit: Kiichiro Sato)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly 2 years as energy costs spike
item.description: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.
item.content:encoded:
Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: VP Vance to lead U.S. team in Iran peace talks. And, Artemis II to return to Earth
item.description: Vice President Vance is heading to Pakistan to lead a U.S. team in talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. And, Artemis II is set to return to Earth today.
item.content:encoded:
Vice President Vance is heading to Pakistan to lead a U.S. team in talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. And, Artemis II is set to return to Earth today.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: China's Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of key summit with Trump
item.description: Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.
item.content:encoded:
Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.

(Image credit: Xie Huanchi)
item.dc:creator: Ashish Valentine
